artifact.content_preview:
From: j.henderson@techsupplier.ca
To: alice.tran@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Cc: bob.morrison@ssc-spc.gc.ca; ngis-pm@ssc-spc.gc.ca
Subject: RE: NGIS Phase 3 — Network Equipment Delivery Update
Date: Mon, 2 Mar 2026 09:14:32 -0500

Alice,

Following our call on Friday, I wanted to confirm in writing the situation
with the Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches for the NGIS Phase 3 data centre
migration.

Unfortunately, our primary distribution centre experienced a warehouse
management system failure that has affected ou

extracted_tasks.text: please confirm the delivery timeline for the Catalyst
extracted_tasks.source_excerpt: please confirm the delivery timeline for the Catalyst

metadata.subject: RE: NGIS Phase 3 — Network Equipment Delivery Update

risks.description: Confirmed 2-week delay in delivery of Cisco Catalyst 9500 switches pushes arrival to March 20, after the March 8 migration window opens, making it impossible to meet the March 6 staging/testing deadline and likely causing the Q2 migration milestone to slip.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We have now confirmed a 2-week delay on the network equipment originally scheduled to arrive at your Gatineau facility on March 6. The revised estimated delivery date is March 20, 2026.

risks.description: Single-source dependency on TechSupplier's Montreal warehouse for the Cisco Catalyst 9500 product line means there is no rapid alternative supply path; the only fallback (Vancouver depot) adds a further 3–5 business days, deepening the delay rather than mitigating it.

evidence_refs.excerpt: We are currently single-sourced on this product line through our Montreal warehouse, and alternative routing via our Vancouver depot would add a further 3–5 business days.

risks.description: The root cause of the delay is a warehouse management system (WMS) failure at the supplier's primary distribution centre, which is an ongoing IT outage of unknown resolution timeline; if the WMS is not restored promptly, the March 20 revised date is itself at risk of further slippage.

evidence_refs.excerpt: Our primary distribution centre experienced a warehouse management system failure that has affected outbound shipments since February 26.

risks.description: Cascading resource and cost risk: SSC technical staff, contractors, and the Gatineau facility migration window are likely pre-booked around the March 8 start date. A forced reschedule may incur idle labour costs, change-order fees, and potential contractual penalties if the Q2 migration commitment is tied to government service-level obligations.

evidence_refs.excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline.

risks.description: Implicit compliance and governance risk: NGIS Phase 3 is a Government of Canada shared-services infrastructure program likely subject to Treasury Board project gating and departmental service continuity requirements. A delayed migration may breach committed service transition dates, triggering mandatory reporting, ministerial briefings, or audit scrutiny.
affected_stakeholders: client departments dependent on NGIS infrastructure

evidence_refs.excerpt: I understand this creates significant pressure on your Q2 migration timeline.
